seed_problem: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: replay the online spot-vs-on-demand provisioning decision as a STATIC full-trace batch-scheduling problem, AND restrict the output with per-step throughput caps + a per-step activation (setup) cost so cheap-spot packing must be traded off against setup amortization -> the offline optimum becomes NP-hard (scheduling unit jobs with release/deadline windows and setup costs) rather than a per-step greedy.
title: Warehouse Robot Charging: Spot / On-Demand / Pause Replay
strategies: trivial baseline: earliest-deadline-first pack -- sort tasks by deadline and place each at the earliest in-window step with spare throughput, ignoring cost entirely
cost-aware marginal greedy: process tasks in deadline order and place each at the in-window step minimizing the marginal cost delta (spot price / grid overflow / new activation) at that moment

local-search consolidation: start from the cheaper of the EDF and greedy schedules, then repeatedly move single tasks to a cheaper in-window step (amortizing activations, filling cheap spot capacity) until no improving move remains, over several fixed-seed restarts
invalid: assign every task to step 1, violating windows and throughput
